clusters.title: Three dead after car slams into tree in Perth
clusters.neutral_headline: Three dead after car slams into tree in Perth
bullet_summary: Reported by 9News, The Age, ABC News Australia

items.title: Three dead after car slams into tree in Perth
items.standfirst: T hree adults inside the car died at the scene.
items.content: T hree people have died after a car slammed into a tree in Perth.Police were called to Stoneville Road in Stoneville to the north-east of the city at about 8.50am after a car left the road and hit a tree.Three adults inside all died at the scene, police say.LIVE UPDATES: Worrying development days after China helped broker ceasefireMore to come. ...NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: Three people dead after single vehicle car crash in Perth Hills
items.standfirst: Police and ambulances ran to the scene in Stoneville just before 9am this morning but the occupants of the vehicle could not be saved.
items.content: Police and ambulances ran to the scene in Stoneville just before 9am this morning but the occupants of the vehicle could not be saved.

items.content: Three people have been killed in a horror crash in Stoneville after a car left the road and ploughed into a tree.

items.content: Three people have died after a vehicle left the road and hit a tree in the Perth Hills suburb of Stoneville.

clusters.title: Qantas flight diverted after strong smell reported
clusters.neutral_headline: Qantas flight diverted after strong smell reported

items.title: Qantas flight diverted after strong smell reported
items.standfirst: A Qantas plane bound for Perth landed unexpectedly at Adelaide Airport with paramedics on standby after passengers and crew noticed a strong smell.
items.content: A Qantas plane bound for Perth landed unexpectedly at Adelaide Airport with paramedics on standby after passengers and crew noticed a strong smell.Paramedics were on standby as the plane landed just before midnight, breaking city's curfew but landed safely at about 11.40pm yesterday.They were called to assess about 15 passengers who had potentially inhaled carbon monoxide but they were given the all clear.READ MORE: Wife breaks down after kind-hearted husband killed in 'vicious' shooting Due to AFL's Gather Round, spare hotel rooms were scare in the city, forcing many of the passengers to spend the night in the Qantas lounge before taking the next flight to Perth in the morning. The flight was flying from Canberra to Perth when crew and passengers noticed a mechanical smell onboard and the plane was forced to divert and land in Adelaide."They turned everything off to try find where it was coming from, couldn't find where it was coming from." one passenger said.READ MORE: Kettle recalled over glaring design fault The aircraft wasn't grounded for long with Qantas engineers assessing the plane overnight.It's already back in the air and was carrying passengers earlier today.Qantas apologised for the inconvenience. NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
